methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Reflect Orbital is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Ben Nowack and Tristan Semmelhack that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis employs two applicable triangulation methods against a peer set of 5 private companies at the same or adjacent stage (ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Sift, Stoke Space, and Aalyria) and 5 public comparables in Space & Aerospace (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-round progression, yielding $40M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies produces $400M, with the comparable set ranging from $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria); notably, ClearSpace—the closest peer by stage—is valued at $400M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure for the target. The triangulated valuation range is $34M (low) to $460M (high) with a median of $220M. Key uncertainties include Reflect Orbital's revenue traction relative to the peer cohort, the wide dispersion among Series B comparables ($274M–$1.3B), and the absence of public-market-derived pricing signals. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects typical early-stage variance.
